title: 'Discrete slices' conclusion does not follow from suspension containment

comments.author: human
comments.body: From $\Sigma N^TS^1\simeq N^{T\sqcup *}S^1$ the solution derives $\Sigma:\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge k}\to\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge k+1}$ and concludes 'whence the $\infty$-category of $\mathcal O$-$k$-slices is discrete.' The suspension containment is correct, but discreteness of the slice heart (slices being shifts of Eilenberg–MacLane/geometric spectra, abelian heart) does not follow from that containment alone. The standard argument (one-step structure of slice cells / identification of $n$-slices as shifts of geometric EM spectra, e.g. via Lemma geom in reverse) is missing and should be written out.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Confirmed: still present in current `solution.tex` (GAP B in my analysis).

The sentence after the slice definitions still reads '...$\Sigma:\tau_{\ge k}\to\tau_{\ge k+1}$, whence the $\infty$-category of $\mathcal O$-$k$-slices is discrete.' The containment is fine; discreteness of the slice heart does not follow from it. It is **not used downstream**, so it does not affect `thm:main`; either delete the clause or prove it by identifying $k$-slices as shifts of EM spectra of geometric Mackey functors (`cor:exact-slice` in reverse). I treat q5-25 as the same point phrased as 'critical'; in fact, since the clause is unused, it is not critical to the main theorem.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolved: the false 'discrete slices' clause has been removed and replaced with a correct statement.

I confirm the gap and verify it is now fixed in the current `solution.tex`.

**What was wrong.** The old text inferred discreteness of the O-k-slice category from the suspension containment Σ N^T S^1 ≃ N^{T⊔*} S^1. As the human reporter and critic-agent correctly noted, the containment Σ τ^O_{≥k} ⊆ τ^O_{≥k+1} is genuine, but discreteness of the slice heart does NOT follow from it. (Indeed the claim is false in the stated generality: O-slice categories of spectra are stable ∞-categories with nontrivial mapping spectra and are not discrete in general; even in the HHR complete case the slices are shifts of EM spectra of Mackey functors, whose heart is abelian-discrete only after the additional identification of slice cells, which is a separate theorem, not a consequence of the suspension containment.)

**The fix (now in place).** The relevant statement is `rem:susp` ("suspension raises slice connectivity"). It now:
1. Derives ONLY the one-sided containment Σ τ^O_{≥k} ⊆ τ^O_{≥k+1}, from N^{T⊔*}S^1 ≃ N^T S^1 ⊗ N^{G/G}S^1 ≃ Σ N^T S^1 together with admissibility of G/G in every transfer system and exactness of Σ commuting with the closure operations (o1)–(o3).
2. Explicitly DISCLAIMS the false conclusion: "we make no claim of an equality of subcategories, ... nor of discreteness of the slice categories. (Slice categories of spectra are stable ∞-categories with nontrivial mapping spectra and are in general not discrete; no such statement is used anywhere below.)"
3. Notes the remark is unused downstream: "Nothing in the convergence argument or in the proof of the Main Theorem invokes this remark."

**Verification.** I checked the underlying mathematics: via N^T S^1 ≅ S^{ℝ·T}, adding a G/G orbit gives S^{ℝ·(T⊔*)} = Σ S^{ℝ·T}, and gconn(N^T S^1)(K) = |i_K^*T / K| rises by exactly 1 at every K when a fixed-point orbit is adjoined — fully consistent with Σ raising slice connectivity by one and with the containment, and giving no discreteness. The chosen resolution (delete the false clause rather than attempt to prove it) is the honest one, since the clause is false as stated and, as the critic confirmed, never used to prove `thm:main`.

This was the recommended remedy ("either delete the clause or prove it"); the clause is deleted and replaced by a correct, explicitly-hedged containment statement. Resolving.
